title: The North Wind and the Sun
lines: The North Wind and the Sun were disputing which was the stronger.
"I am, without question," said the North Wind, and to prove it he began to blow with all his might upon a wayfarer.
The harder he blew, the tighter the traveler wrapped his cloak around him.
At last the North Wind gave up the attempt.
Then the Sun began to shine, at first gently, then in all his strength.
Soon the traveler found it too hot, and one by one he began to remove his garments, until at last he was forced to go and dip himself in a cool stream.
"You see," said the Sun, "that I am the stronger."
moral: Kindness and persuasion are more powerful than force.
